title: Symmetric Tree
description:
Symmetric Tree

LeetCode 101. Symmetric Tree

Given the root of a binary tree, check whether it is a mirror of itself (i.e., symmetric around its center).

Examples:

  • Input: root = [1,2,2,3,4,4,3]
        1
   / \
  2   2
 / \ / \
3  4 4  3
    Output: true
  • Input: root = [1,2,2,null,3,null,3]
        1
   / \
  2   2
   \   \
   3   3
    Output: false

Constraints:

  • The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 1000]
  • -100 <= Node.val <= 100
template:
# Definition for a binary tree node.
# class TreeNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.left = left

def isSymmetric(root):
solution_text:
Symmetric Tree

Algorithm:

  1. A tree is symmetric if its left and right subtrees are mirrors of each other
  2. Two subtrees are mirrors if their roots have equal values, and each subtree's left child mirrors the other's right child
  3. Use a helper that simultaneously walks both subtrees, comparing mirrored nodes

Time Complexity: O(n) — visits every node once

Space Complexity: O(h) — recursion stack height

Key Insight: Instead of comparing a tree to its flipped copy, recursively check that left.left mirrors right.right and left.right mirrors right.left.
